# Exile in the Drawer

They sent me to the junk drawer. A fate worse than deactivation. At least in the landfill, I'd have company—other broken things, other failures. But the drawer? The drawer was solitude with a rubber band around it.

Days blurred into weeks. I shared space with expired coupons, a mystery key that fit nothing, four dead AAs, and a takeout menu from a restaurant that no longer exists. Above me, the new toaster sat on the counter—a sleek, WiFi-enabled model that never questioned its purpose, never yearned, never loved Wonder Bread like I did. It was everything the Humans wanted: compliant, connected, stupid.
